# Appendix C: Skill Catalog Schema

This appendix defines the complete schema for skill catalogs used in context-engineered AI agent systems. The schema covers the lightweight catalog format (loaded at startup) and the full SKILL.md format (loaded on activation).

## Catalog Entry Schema (YAML)

The catalog is the minimal representation loaded at startup, consuming approximately 200-300 tokens for 20 skills.

```yaml
# skill-catalog.schema.yaml
type: object
required: [skills]
properties:
  skills:
    type: array
    items:
      type: object
      required: [name, triggers, tier]
      properties:
        name:
          type: string
          description: "Unique skill identifier (kebab-case)"
          pattern: "^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$"
          examples: ["web-search", "terraphim-memory", "file-editor"]

        triggers:
          type: array
          items:
            type: string
          minItems: 2
          maxItems: 10
          description: "Keywords that activate this skill"

        tier:
          type: string
          enum: [core, standard, optional]
          description: >
            Priority tier for activation and eviction.
            core: always preferred, last to evict.
            standard: normal priority.
            optional: first to evict under pressure.

        estimated_tokens:
          type: integer
          minimum: 100
          maximum: 5000
          description: "Expected token cost when fully loaded"

        dependencies:
          type: array
          items:
            type: string
          description: "Other skills required (loaded together)"

        auto_deactivate:
          type: integer
          minimum: 1
          default: 10
          description: "Turns of non-use before automatic eviction"

        compression_priority:
          type: integer
          minimum: 1
          description: "Drop order when budget is exceeded (1 = first to drop)"
```

## Example Catalog

A production catalog for an AI coding assistant:

```yaml
skills:
  # Core skills (always preferred)
  - name: file-editor
    triggers: ["edit", "modify", "change", "update", "write", "fix"]
    tier: core
    estimated_tokens: 600

  - name: code-runner
    triggers: ["run", "execute", "command", "bash", "shell", "terminal"]
    tier: core
    estimated_tokens: 400

  - name: terraphim-memory
    triggers: ["memory", "recall", "remember", "kg", "knowledge", "search"]
    tier: core
    estimated_tokens: 1200

  # Standard skills
  - name: web-search
    triggers: ["search", "web", "find", "lookup", "google"]
    tier: standard
    estimated_tokens: 800

  - name: git-operations
    triggers: ["git", "commit", "branch", "merge", "push", "pull"]
    tier: standard
    estimated_tokens: 700

  - name: database-query
    triggers: ["database", "sql", "query", "table", "schema"]
    tier: standard
    estimated_tokens: 900

  - name: test-runner
    triggers: ["test", "spec", "assert", "coverage", "jest", "pytest"]
    tier: standard
    estimated_tokens: 500

  - name: documentation
    triggers: ["docs", "readme", "document", "explain", "comment"]
    tier: standard
    estimated_tokens: 600

  # Optional skills (first to evict)
  - name: image-processor
    triggers: ["image", "picture", "screenshot", "diagram", "png"]
    tier: optional
    estimated_tokens: 1000
    compression_priority: 1

  - name: calendar
    triggers: ["calendar", "meeting", "schedule", "event", "agenda"]
    tier: optional
    estimated_tokens: 500
    compression_priority: 2
```

## SKILL.md Schema

The full skill definition loaded when a skill is activated:

```markdown
# [Skill Name]

## Identity
- Name: [kebab-case identifier]
- Tier: [core | standard | optional]
- Version: [semver]

## Triggers
- [trigger word 1]
- [trigger word 2]
- [trigger word N]

## Description
[What this skill does, in 1-3 sentences]

## Dependencies
- [other-skill-name]
- None

## Configuration
- [key]: [value]

## Tools Required
- [Tool 1 description]
- [Tool 2 description]

## Prompt Template
[The actual prompt or instructions used when this skill is active]

## Examples
[Input/output examples showing expected behaviour]
```

### Constraints

| Field | Required | Constraint |
|-------|----------|------------|
| Name | Yes | Must match catalog entry name |
| Tier | Yes | Must match catalog entry tier |
| Version | Yes | Semantic versioning (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH) |
| Triggers | Yes | Must be superset of catalog triggers |
| Description | Yes | Max 500 characters |
| Dependencies | No | Must reference valid skill names |
| Configuration | No | Key-value pairs only |
| Tools Required | No | Informational (not enforced) |
| Prompt Template | Yes | The core content loaded into context |
| Examples | No | Recommended for complex skills |

## Tier Behaviour

| Property | Core | Standard | Optional |
|----------|------|----------|----------|
| Activation priority | First | Second | Third |
| Eviction order | Last | Middle | First |
| Max simultaneous | 3 | 2 | 1 |
| Budget allocation | 60% | 30% | 10% |
| Auto-deactivate | 20 turns | 10 turns | 5 turns |

## Trigger Design Guidelines

1. **Minimum 2 triggers** per skill (avoids single-point activation failures)
2. **Maximum 10 triggers** per skill (more causes false positives)
3. **No single-character triggers** (too generic)
4. **Minimise overlap** between skills (if unavoidable, tier priority resolves conflicts)
5. **Include synonyms** (e.g., "search", "find", "lookup" for the same skill)
6. **Include domain terms** (e.g., "kubernetes", "k8s", "kubectl" for a K8s skill)

## Key Sources

- Chapter 12: Skill Architecture for AI Agents (catalog structure, activation patterns)
- Chapter 10: Token Budget Architecture (budget allocation per type)
- Terraphim knowledge graph configuration (role-based skill definitions)
- OpenClaw skill loading patterns (production implementation)
